passages.title: Computer Games For Preschoolers:Nintendo's Research And Design Process
passages.source_html:
Computer Games for Preschoolers: Nintendo's Research and Design Process


Designing video games for the youngest consumers is a difficult task for producers, who historically have focused on more 'hard core' game fans. This article describes the research and design process involved in creating Nintendo DS* games for a preschool audience.


A


After consulting with companies who were interested in designing for the Nintendo DS, we decided on three key objectives for our initial exploratory project: understand preschoolers' abilities in the context of handheld system game play; understand how preschoolers interact with the Nintendo DS, specifically how they handle the various forms of play and game mechanics offered by the games currently on the market for this system; and understand the expectations of their parents with regard to handheld systems. The research team began by conducting 26 in-home interviews with children in three markets across the US (West coast urban/suburban; Midwest suburban/rural; and East coast urban/suburban).


B


The preschoolers in this study included 11 boys and 15 girls ranging in age from 3 years and 3 months to 5 years and 11 months. In addition, because previous research we conducted had shown the effects of school-age siblings on game play (demonstrated, for example, by more advanced motor coordination when using a computer mouse), a combination of households was recruited which had preschoolers with and without school-age siblings. In addition, in order to understand both experienced preschool users of the system and those who were new to video games, we split the sample so that thirteen families owned at least one Nintendo DS and thirteen did not. For those households that did not already have a Nintendo DS, one was brought to the interview for the child to play. This allowed us to see both the instinctive movements of the new players and of the more experienced players when playing new games), as well as the learned movements of the more experienced players. Each of these interviews lasted between one and two hours and included the preschooler, at least one parent, and often siblings and another parent or caregiver.


C


During each interview various questions were asked. From the parents and any older siblings that were available, we found out, where relevant, the purchase decisions surrounding game systems in the household; the family's typical game play habits; levels of parental supervision with regard to gaming; and favorite games played by various family members. Because these interviews were in-home, we were also able to understand the gaming culture in these homes: what types of spaces (communal or private) were used for game play; how the systems were set up; where the handheld play occurred in the house; and the number and type of games and game systems owned. Finally, and most importantly, we talked to each child about their game playing.


D


Prior to the interviews, the research team had worked with the production team to create a list of issues connected with preschoolers physical and mental abilities in the context of playing games. It was essential to understand these before developing the games. These ranged from manual dexterity issues related to game controls, to the effectiveness of in-game instructions, to specific operating techniques used in current games that the producers were interested in incorporating in future preschool titles. During the interviews, therefore, the interviewer guided the preschooler through a series of games, so that he/she could observe the interaction, and question both the preschooler and his/her parents on feelings, attitudes, and frustrations that arose during play.


E


If the child had previous experience with the Nintendo DS, he/she was first asked to play his/her favorite game on that system. This told the researchers not only about current level of gaming experience, particularly as related to the complexity of the chosen game, but also allowed them to see the child playing a game using procedures he/she was already familiar with. Across the group of preschoolers, the Nintendo DS selections were very broad, including Nintendo’s, New Super Mario Bros, Tony Hawk's Proving Ground, and Sonic Rush. The interviewer watched the child play, specifically noting preferences for operating procedures and physical interactions with the device, as well as how easy or hard each operating procedure was for the child. All of the preschoolers were then asked to play with a specific game chosen by our research team in consultation with our producers, The Little Mermaid: Ariel's Undersea Adventure. It was chosen because it was one of the few games on the market with characters that all of this young age group really liked. Also, it incorporated a large variety of operating techniques that highlighted the uniqueness of the Nintendo DS system, including blowing or singing into the microphone.


F


The findings from this initial exploratory study were extensive. After reviewing the results in-house and discussing the implications for game design, we then outlined our needs to a firm specializing in children's games. We worked intensively with them to base the design for two preschool-targeted Nintendo DS games on what we had learned.


G


As the two Nintendo DS games went into development, we began doing formative research. Whenever we developed new game mechanics, we brought preschoolers into our lab to try them, so we could assess not only their simplicity, but also how engaging they were. Once a full version of the Nintendo DS games was ready, we went back into the field and tested them with a dozen preschoolers, to make sure that each element of the games worked for the children, and that overall, the games were understandable and enjoyable for them. We also got feedback on what their siblings and parents thought about the games with regard to appropriateness, engagement, and purchase-intent.


* Nintendo DS: a portable video games system

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1-5


Complete the sentences. Write ONE WORD ONLY in each gap.

1

Exploratory Research Project


Main Objectives:

• determine the relevant   of preschool children

• observe how preschoolers manage existing games

正确答案： 1 abilities
Question 1

定位关键信息：exploratory project, main objectives


原文：A段，“...we decided on three key objectives for our initial exploratory project: understand preschoolers' abilities in the context of handheld system game play;...”


解析：原文提到项目有三个关键目标，第1个是了解学龄前儿童在游戏中表现出的能力，因此答案为abilities。


同义替换：main=key, determine=understand


2

• investigate attitudes of preschoolers' to games

正确答案： 2 parents
Question 2

定位关键信息：并列关系“;”以及attitudes


原文：A段，“...; and understand the expectations of their parents with regard to handheld systems.”


解析：“;”对应并列关系。原文提到第3个目标是了解学龄前儿童父母的预期，因此答案为parents。


同义替换：attitudes=expectations, investigate=understand


3

Subjects:

• 26 children from different US

• age range: 3 years 3 months – 5 years 11 months

正确答案： 3 markets
Question 3

定位关键信息：subjects, 26, US


原文：A段，“The research team began by conducting 26 in-home interviews with children in three markets across the US (West coast urban/suburban; Midwest suburban/rural; and East coast urban/suburban).”


解析：原文提到研究团队采访了全美3个地区城市和郊区的26个孩子，因此答案为markets。


同义替换：different=three

4

• some children have older  

正确答案： 4 siblings
Question 4

定位关键信息：3 years 3 months – 5 years 11 months以及并列关系


原文：B段，“In addition, because previous research we conducted had shown the effects of school-age siblings on game play (demonstrated, for example, by more advanced motor coordination when using a computer mouse), a combination of households was recruited which had preschoolers with and without school-age siblings.”


解析：In addition对应并列关系。原文提到因为之前研究表明学龄哥哥姐姐对于学龄前儿童玩游戏的影响，所以这次研究同时招募了有学龄哥哥姐姐的和没有学龄哥哥姐姐的家庭。


同义替换：older=school-age

5

• equal number of new and  players

• some households with Nintendo DS and some without


Length of Interview:

• 1-2 hours

正确答案： 5 experienced
Question 5

定位关键信息：equal numbers, new以及并列关系

原文：B段，“In addition, in order to understand both experienced preschool users of the system and those who were new to video games, we split the sample so that thirteen families owned at least one Nintendo DS and thirteen did not.”

 

解析：In addition对应并列关系。原文提到项目同时研究了熟悉电子游戏和游戏新手的学龄前儿童，因此答案为experienced。

 

同义替换：equal numbers=thirteen

Questions 6 - 9


Choose TRUE if the statement agrees with the information, choose FALSE if the statement contradicts the information, or choose NOT GIVEN if there is no information on this

6

One of the areas investigated by researchers was how far mothers or fathers controlled their children's game playing.

正确答案： A. TRUE

Q6，TRUE

定位关键信息：mothers，fathers


原文：C段，“During each interview various questions were asked. From the parents and any older siblings that were available, we found out, where relevant, the purchase decisions surrounding game systems in the household; the family's typical game play habits; levels of parental supervision with regard to gaming; and favorite games played by various family members.”


解析：原文提到采访时会问很多问题，其中就包括父母对孩子玩游戏的监管程度。这句话与题目表达意思相同，因此答案为TRUE。


同义替换：how far mothers or fathers controlled=levels of parental supervision

7

The researchers regarded The Little Mermaid: Ariel's Undersea Adventure as likely to appeal to preschoolers.

正确答案： A. TRUE

Q7，TRUE

定位关键信息：The Little Mermaid: Ariel's Undersea Adventure


原文：E段，“It was chosen because it was one of the few games on the market with characters that all of this young age group really liked.”


解析：原文提到研究者选择这个游戏是因为它是含有所有学龄前儿童都喜欢角色。这句话与题目表达意思相同，因此答案为TRUE。


同义替换：appeal=like, this young age group=preschoolers

8

The researchers were allowed free access to all parts of the subjects' houses.

正确答案： C. NOT GIVEN

Q8，NOT GIVEN

定位关键信息：free access, subjects' house


解析：全文并未提及研究人员被允许随意进入研究对象的家中，因此答案为NOT GIVEN。

9

The Little Mermaid: Ariel's Undersea Adventure game is operated entirely by hand controls.

正确答案： B. FALSE

Q9，FALSE

定位关键信息：The Little Mermaid: Ariel's Undersea Adventure, by hand


原文：E段，“Also, it incorporated a large variety of operating techniques that highlighted the uniqueness of the Nintendo DS system, including blowing or singing into the microphone.”


解析：原文提到这款游戏包含了大量Nintendo DS独有的操作方式，如对着麦克风吹气和唱歌。这句话与题目中的“完全用手控制 operated entirely by hand”相矛盾，因此答案为FALSE。



﻿Questions 10-13


Complete the flow-chart below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS in each gap.

10


Using the results of the study


in-house review of results

               ↓

presentation of design requirements to a specialist

正确答案： 10 firm
Question 10

定位关键信息：review, presentation

原文：G段，“After reviewing the results in-house and discussing the implications for game design, we then outlined our needs to a firm specializing in children's games.”


解析：原文提到在内部审查了这些结果并讨论了它们对游戏设计的影响后，接着会向一家专注于儿童游戏的公司概述需求，因此答案为firm。


同义替换：presentation=outlined, requirements=needs, a firm specializing in children's games= a specialist firm


11

               ↓

mechanics of two new games in the Nintendo DS lab (assess for and interest)

正确答案： 11 simplicity
Question 11

定位关键信息：review, presentation

原文：G段，“... we brought preschoolers into our lab to try them, so we could assess not only their simplicity, but also how engaging they were.”

 

解析：原文提到研究人员评估游戏的简单程度和吸引力，因此答案为

simplicity。

 

同义替换：trial=assess，interest=engaging

12

               ↓

a of each game trialled with twelve children

正确答案： 12 full version
Question 12

定位关键信息：trial, twelve

原文：G段，“Once a full version of the Nintendo DS games was ready, we went back into the field and tested them with a dozen preschoolers,...”

 

解析：原文提到Nintendo DS游戏最终版本都会有12个学龄前儿童参与测试，因此答案为full version/version

 

同义替换：trial=test, twelve=dozen


13

               ↓

collection of   from family members

正确答案： 13 feedback
Question 13

定位关键信息：collection, family members

原文：G段，“We also got feedback on what their siblings and parents thought about the games with regard to appropriateness, engagement, and purchase-intent.”


解析：原文提到研究者收集参与测试者的兄弟姐妹以及父母对于游戏看法的反馈，因此答案为feedback。


同义替换：collection=get, family members=siblings and parents
